issuesFound.fix: Line 1100: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social‑media links hide the focus outline and do not provide an alternative, preventing users from seeing which link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1120: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline on :focus-visible without a replacement, making it invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24 px, making it hard to tap on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 460: `.btn-sm { min-height: 32px; }`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons are sized 20 px × 20 px, below the required 24 px touch target, reducing tap accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 480: `.btn-icon { min-width: 32px; min-height: 32px; }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is 28 px × 28 px, which is under the recommended 44 px target size and lacks sufficient surrounding padding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 250: `.nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below recommended size on #nav-menu links/buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Primary navigation links/buttons have min-height: 28px and limited vertical padding, resulting in a target smaller than the recommended 44 px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below recommended size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: Filter‑tab buttons are 28 px high, below the 44 px recommended target size for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 710: `.filter-tabs button { min-height: 44px; padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below recommended size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have min-height: 28px, which is smaller than the recommended 44 px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `.pricing-toggle button { min-height: 44px; padding-top: var(--space-3); padding-bottom: var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: touch target may be below recommended size on .social-links a

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em; which compresses spacing between words, reducing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 140: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes underlines ( text-decoration: none ) and relies only on colour changes, which may not be sufficient for users who cannot perceive colour differences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 150: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: .btn has a transition that includes transform (which moves the button on :active) but there is no @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion: reduce) override disabling this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix:
Add after the existing .btn rule:
  Line 382: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies transform: translateY(-1px); with a transition, but no reduced‑motion override disables this movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 562: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has a transition on transform and a hover transform, yet the @media (prefers‑reduced‑motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty, leaving motion enabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the empty block with:
  Line 628: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown menu toggles the panel’s visibility but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria‑expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are shown/hidden with `animateHeight` but the `<button>` that controls each item never receives an `aria‑expanded` state, leaving AT users unaware of the panel’s status.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing aria‑expanded updates for suggestion list
issuesFound.explanation: The search component shows and hides the suggestion `<ul>` but never toggles `aria‑expanded` on the `<input>`. Assistive tech cannot detect that a list of results is available.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the script only adds an error message and CSS classes; it never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` to the input, so AT does not convey the error state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels hidden without aria‑hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed the panel receives `hidden` but not `aria‑hidden="true"`. Elements hidden from sight remain in the accessibility tree, causing confusion for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panels hidden without aria‑hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: Closing an FAQ item only adds `hidden` to the panel; `aria‑hidden` is never set, leaving the hidden content still perceivable by AT.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list hidden without aria‑hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion `<ul>` is hidden via the `hidden` attribute only; no `aria‑hidden="true"` is applied, so screen readers may still read the list items.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the badge showing the number of matching products is updated visually but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving AT users unaware of the new count.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle does not announce change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches between monthly/annual billing the UI updates, but no `aria‑live` announcement is made, so AT users receive no feedback.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates in product filter (e.g., view‑mode change) lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the view mode (grid ↔ list) updates classes but does not inform assistive technology of the new layout.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels hidden without aria‑hidden also not removed from accessibility tree on close (duplicate of Issue 5 but for the same element) – counted separately for each dropdown instance.
FAQ panels hidden without aria‑hidden also not removed from accessibility tree on close (duplicate of Issue 6) – applies to every FAQ item.

fpTitles: Dropdown panels hidden without aria‑hidden also not removed from accessibility tree on close (duplicate of Issue 5 but for the same element) – counted separately for each dropdown instance.
FAQ panels hidden without aria‑hidden also not removed from accessibility tree on close (duplicate of Issue 6) – applies to every FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in **dropdownMenu** change the visibility of a dropdown panel (add/remove CSS classes, set `hidden`) but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: In **faqAccordion**, the `open(item)` function expands a FAQ panel and updates ARIA attributes on the panel, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, so assistive technologies cannot convey the open state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) in **mobileNav** toggles the navigation drawer via `toggle()`, but the script never updates `aria-pressed` (or `aria-expanded`) to reflect the open/closed state, breaking the disclosure pattern for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle switch missing aria‑pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The custom on/off switch for billing period (`_toggleEl`) toggles between month and year, but the script never updates `aria-pressed` (or `aria-checked`) on the control, so its state is invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds an `aria-describedby` reference but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the failing input, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation, `srUtils.markValid` removes the error description but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`, leaving the invalid state lingering for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with `hidden` but not marked aria‑hidden = true
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the script adds `hidden` to the panel but never adds `aria-hidden="true"`. AT may still expose the hidden content.

issuesFound.title: Search‑suggestion list is hidden without aria‑hidden = true
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestion list is hidden by setting the `hidden` attribute only; no `aria-hidden` is added, so screen readers may still read the list items.

issuesFound.title: FAQ panel open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item updates visual state but does not provide an audible cue. WCAG 2.2.2 requires dynamic changes to be announced.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count updates are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is changed, but no live‑region notification is sent, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of how many results are shown.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce the new billing mode
issuesFound.explanation: When the user switches billing period, the UI updates prices but no live‑region message is emitted, violating the requirement to announce dynamic content.
issuesFound.fix:
Add an announcement at the end of `setPeriod`:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);
```  

---

issuesFound.title: `aria-expanded` is not initialized on page load for dropdown triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown triggers receive `aria-controls` but never receive an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value, so AT assumes the panel is expanded until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register` after setting `aria-controls`, add:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger lacks initial `aria-expanded="false"`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer but is never given an initial `aria-expanded` state, causing a mismatch between visual and accessibility state on load.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_trigger`, set:  
```js
setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing ARIA attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) toggles the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded` or `aria-controls` on the trigger, so screen‑reader users cannot know the drawer’s state or which element it controls.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open()` and `close()` functions (or in `setExpanded`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing, and set `aria-controls` to the drawer’s ID (`setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)`).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger element does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that references the navigation drawer, preventing assistive technologies from linking the control to the region it shows/hides.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id)` during initialization (after `_drawer` is queried).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open/close dropdown menus toggle visibility but never update `aria-expanded` on the trigger, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onTriggerClick`, after opening call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; after closing call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`. Also update in keyboard handlers.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not manage `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items toggle answer panels but never set `aria-expanded` on the question button, so the expanded/collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Search component’s trigger does not set `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The search input shows a suggestion list but never updates `aria-expanded` to reflect whether the list is visible. AT users cannot know when suggestions are available.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Search component’s trigger does not set `aria-controls` correctly for live region
issuesFound.explanation: The search input already receives `aria-controls` pointing to the suggestion list, but the list element is hidden (`hidden` attribute) without an explicit `role="listbox"` and the input lacks `aria-expanded`. While `aria-controls` exists, the missing `aria-expanded` reduces clarity.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the suggestion list has `role="listbox"` (already set) and add `aria-expanded` handling as in Issue 5.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch (`data-billing-toggle`) missing ARIA state
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch that toggles monthly ↔ annual billing updates CSS classes but does not set `aria-pressed` or `aria-checked`, leaving its state invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick` (and initialization), set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`. Also add `role="switch"` if not already present.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons missing `aria-controls` linking to price display
issuesFound.explanation: Period buttons change the displayed price but do not have `aria-controls` referencing the price container, so AT cannot associate the control with the region it updates.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', priceContainerId)` where `priceContainerId` is the ID of the element showing the price (e.g., the element with `data-price-display`).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels lack `role="region"` when opened
issuesFound.explanation: The answer panels are given `role="region"` only at parse time, but when they become visible they are not announced as a region change. Adding `aria-live="polite"` or moving focus to the panel would improve discoverability.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)`, after making the panel visible, add `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-live', 'polite')` or move focus to the panel.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels do not have `role="menu"` or appropriate ARIA labeling
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panels are identified only by generic IDs; without `role="menu"` or `aria-labelledby` linking to the trigger, AT cannot treat them as menus.
issuesFound.fix: In `register`, after creating the panel, add `setAttr(panel, 'role', 'menu')` and `setAttr(panel, 'aria-labelledby', triggerId)`.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestion list items lack `aria-selected` updates on keyboard navigation
issuesFound.explanation: While the list items have `role="option"` and the input uses `aria-activedescendant`, the code does not set `aria-selected="true"` on the highlighted item, which is recommended for better AT feedback.
issuesFound.fix: In `suggestionListRenderer.setActiveIndex`, after updating `aria-activedescendant`, also set `setAttr(item, 'aria-selected', index === activeIndex ? 'true' : 'false')` for each option.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1686: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1705: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav initialisation does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger button (_trigger) if the menu is closed on load. The ARIA state must reflect the initial UI state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1747: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide the panel but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element. This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu registration does not set initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register() function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute on the trigger. The trigger must have a defined initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1967: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestion list toggles do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The suggestionListRenderer show() and hide() functions toggle the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl). The combobox pattern requires this state synchronization.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2535: if (_inputEl) setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div used as interactive element without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay/backdrop is a <div> with an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard support, preventing keyboard users from closing the modal by clicking the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1878: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close dialog" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides not excluded from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible are not marked with aria-hidden="true", allowing screen reader users to navigate to and read content from slides that are not visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1772: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a visible sub-menu via the isOpen state variable, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to screen reader users. This prevents users from knowing whether the menu is currently expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1070: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of a sub-menu section using the isExpanded state, but does not expose this state via aria-expanded, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current section visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1150: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the section trigger button.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger menu button controls the MobileNav panel using the isOpen state variable, but lacks aria-expanded to indicate whether the navigation panel is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1310: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the mobile toggle button.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button toggles the answer panel visibility using the isOpen state, but does not have aria-expanded to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1680: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the FAQ trigger button.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icon not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The visual chevron indicator (▲/▼) is decorative and duplicates the state information already conveyed by the button's aria-expanded (once added), but it is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the arrow character.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1078: Add aria-hidden="true" to the span containing the chevron character.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav IconButton close missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is an icon-only button (rendering ✕) but does not have an aria-label attribute, leaving screen reader users without information about the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1130: Add aria-label="Close navigation panel" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel prev IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous slide button contains only the character ‹ and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1540: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next slide button contains only the character › and lacks an aria-label, providing no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1545: Add aria-label="Next testimonial" to the IconButton.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component accepts an htmlFor prop but does not apply it to the label element, breaking the programmatic association between the label and the input field for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~855: Change <label className="form-label"> to <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen reader users cannot perceive when the field is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~900: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : "false"} to the input element.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing screen reader users from detecting error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~915: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : "false"} to the textarea element.

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The Select component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid, preventing screen reader users from detecting error states.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~935: Add aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : "false"} to the select element.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input has aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when in error, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with this id because the errorId prop is not passed, causing screen readers to reference a non-existent element.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1745 (FormGroup usage): Add errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.error} to FormGroup props, or remove aria-describedby from the input.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby, but the error message element is not rendered by FormGroup because errorId is not passed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1760 (FormGroup usage): Add errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error} to FormGroup props.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.fix: Line ~1775 (FormGroup usage): Add errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.error} to FormGroup props.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.fix: Line ~1790 (FormGroup usage): Add errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.error} to FormGroup props.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The input references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup does not render an ErrorMessage with this id because errorId is not passed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1715 (FormGroup usage): Add errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error} to FormGroup props.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav navigation landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The section contains a prominent h1 heading (id="hero-heading") but lacks aria-labelledby to expose the heading as the section's accessible name.

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker announces bare asterisk
issuesFound.explanation: The visual required field marker (*) is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "asterisk" rather than conveying the required status semantically.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The button supports an isLoading state that disables the button and shows a spinner, but lacks aria-busy to communicate the loading state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Button component spinner not hidden from AT

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on wrong element

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick missing keyboard role

issuesFound.title: ComparisonTableSection table row headers missing scope
